channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: US-Iran War Cost Reaches $25 Billion as Hormuz Shipping Dwindles to Trickle
item.description: A senior Pentagon official disclosed the first cost estimate for the US war in Iran at $25 billion, while shipping data shows only six vessels crossed the Strait of Hormuz in 24 hours—a fraction of normal traffic—as negotiations remain deadlocked.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: Trump Discusses Ukraine Ceasefire with Putin as War Enters Fourth Year
item.description: President Trump revealed he spoke with Russian President Vladimir Putin about a potential ceasefire in Ukraine, marking a significant diplomatic development as the conflict continues.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Facility 1,500 km from Front Lines
item.description: Ukraine claimed a drone strike on an oil pumping station near the Ural Mountains, demonstrating Kyiv's ability to hit targets deep inside Russia as President Zelenskiy vowed to extend the range of attacks.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: US Sanctions China's Hengli in Major Escalation of Iran Oil Crackdown
item.description: The US Treasury imposed sanctions on China's Hengli Petrochemical for purchasing billions of dollars in Iranian oil, marking a significant escalation in efforts to cut Tehran's oil revenue.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: Federal Reserve Holds Rates in Powell's Likely Last Meeting as Chair
item.description: The Fed kept rates steady amid inflation concerns from the Iran war, with the board's vote showing the deepest divisions since 1992 as Powell's term as chairman nears its end.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: Oil Surges Past $120 as Trump Rejects Iranian Peace Overture
item.description: Benchmark Brent crude surpassed $120 per barrel, reaching war-time highs after President Trump rejected an Iranian proposal to end the conflict that would have deferred nuclear talks.
item.author: Al-Monitor

item.title: Trump Rejects Iran Ceasefire Proposal, Vows Continued Pressure
item.description: President Trump rejected an Iranian proposal to end the war, dismissing offers to lift the US naval blockade and indicating further strikes are planned.
item.author: Middle East Eye

item.title: China May Benefit from UAE OPEC Withdrawal Amid Iran War
item.description: Beijing may gain access to increased oil supply through the UAE leaving OPEC, though Strait of Hormuz disruption remains a serious constraint for Chinese energy security.
item.author: SCMP

item.title: Iranian Officials Leave Canada Before FIFA Congress Over Airport "Insult"
item.description: Top Iranian football officials departed Canada before the FIFA Congress after alleging inappropriate treatment by Canadian airport authorities.
item.author: Al-Monitor

item.title: Sudan Sexual Violence Crisis Worsening, UN Warns
item.description: The UN reports that sexual violence being used as a weapon of war in Sudan has triggered a massive mental health crisis, including cases of rape and shootings of health workers.
item.author: SCMP

item.title: IAEA Chief Says Iran Highly Enriched Uranium Still at Isfahan
item.description: UN nuclear agency head Rafael Grossi confirmed that most of Iran highly enriched uranium remains at the Isfahan complex, amid heightened concerns about nuclear proliferation during the US-Israel conflict with Iran.
item.author: AP News

item.title: Israel Intercepts Gaza Aid Ships Far From Shores
item.description: Israel has begun intercepting and taking control of humanitarian aid ships bound for Gaza far from Israeli shores, according to army radio, as a Gaza-bound flotilla approaches the region.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: US Charges Sinaloa Governor Over Cartel Ties
item.description: The US Justice Department charged the governor of Mexico Sinaloa state, Ruben Rocha, and other officials for alleged involvement with the Sinaloa Cartel, a significant escalation of US anti-cartel action.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: Fed Holds Rates Steady in Most Divided Decision Since 1992
item.description: The Federal Reserve held interest rates unchanged but saw three dissents from officials who no longer support a bias toward rate cuts, amid rising inflation concerns during Powell likely last meeting as chair.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: Europes defense hinges on Ukraine as US commitment wavers
item.description: With doubts growing about Americas commitment to transatlantic allies, Europe must strengthen its common defenses within NATO and the EU. The Economist argues Europe needs Ukraine to fight Russia.
item.author: The Economist

item.title: North Koreas nuclear arsenal outgrowing US missile defenses
item.description: Bloomberg analysis shows North Koreas ascent from rogue state to full-fledged atomic power means it may soon have a globally relevant deterrent, with ICBMs capable of striking the US mainland.
item.author: Bloomberg

item.title: Nigeria charges six with treason over coup plot against president
item.description: Nigeria has charged six men with treason for an attempted coup against President Bola Tinubu last October, court filings show. The case highlights political instability in Africas most populous nation.
item.author: New York Times

item.title: Germany unveils strategy to build Europes strongest military by 2039
item.description: Germany announced its first stand-alone military strategy, intending to become the strongest conventional army in Europe. Defense spending has hit a 36-year high as Berlin boosts infantry and space programs.
item.author: Politico EU

item.title: Magyar's Russian energy phase-out plan for Hungary lacks urgency, analysts warn
item.description: Opposition leader Peter Magyar's proposal to end Hungary's reliance on Russian fossil fuels falls short, risking continued dependence on Moscow under Viktor Orban's government.
item.author: Kyiv Independent

item.title: Egypt concludes major military exercises east of Suez, raising Israeli concerns
item.description: Egypt's defense minister oversaw large-scale war drills near the Suez Canal, with Israeli officials expressing concern about the show of force near their border.
item.author: MSN

item.title: Pentagon signs deal with Google to integrate Gemini AI into defense operations
item.description: The US Defense Department reached an agreement with Google for access to the company's powerful Gemini AI capabilities, expanding military use of artificial intelligence.
item.author: Yahoo News

item.title: South Africa to deport Robert Mugabe's son over firearm conviction
item.description: Bellarmine Mugabe, son of the late Zimbabwean president, faces deportation from South Africa after being fined $36,000 for illegal possession of a firearm.
item.author: MSN

item.title: Trump-Merz Clash Reveals US-Europe Divide on Iran Policy
item.description: A diplomatic clash between Trump and German Chancellor Merz highlights growing transatlantic tensions over handling the Iran conflict and security commitments.
item.author: New York Times

item.title: CNN Maps Strait of Hormuz Shipping Disruption
item.description: CNN published detailed visualizations showing how shipping traffic through the strategic Strait of Hormuz has collapsed since the US-Iran conflict began.
item.author: CNN

item.title: Ukraine Develops Private Air Defense Against Shahed Drones
item.description: Ukraine is building a private air defense network to counter Russian Shahed drone attacks as part of efforts to protect critical infrastructure.
item.author: Reuters
